mappings.rationaleAI: Byzantine art and architecture (Hagia Sophia, icons and mosaics), the iconoclasm controversies, and the empire's cultural legacy directly address arts of antiquity and the Middle Ages in the Western tradition.

Greek mythology from Homer and Hesiod through the tragedians, its codification in art, and its reinterpretation through Roman and Renaissance culture directly addresses arts of ancient Greece in the Western tradition.
The Ramayana's enormous cultural influence across South and Southeast Asia, its multiple versions, and its role in Hindu devotion directly address arts of South Asian peoples.

Journey to the West as one of the Four Great Classical Novels of Chinese literature, blending Buddhist allegory with Confucian and Taoist elements, directly addresses arts of East Asian peoples (China).
Tang Dynasty poetry of Li Bai and Du Fu as the golden age of Chinese literature directly addresses arts of East Asian peoples (China).
The Silk Road's spread of cultural and artistic exchange across Central Asia, connecting China with the Mediterranean, addresses arts of Central Asian peoples.
The Shahnameh as the Persian national epic preserving Iranian mythology and its artistic legacy in Persian miniature painting directly addresses arts of the Islamic peoples and the Arab world.
Prehistoric cave art of the Upper Paleolithic at Lascaux, Chauvet, and Altamira directly addresses arts of the Stone Age peoples.
Neanderthal burial practices suggesting symbolic thought and evidence for tool-making culture address pre-Stone Age artistic expression and the origins of symbolic thinking.
Greek and Roman love poetry from Sappho through Catullus and Ovid directly addresses arts of antiquity in the Western tradition.
Ovid's Metamorphoses and its vast influence on Western art and literature from the Middle Ages through Shakespeare directly addresses arts of ancient Rome in the Western tradition.
Celtic art and metalwork, La Tene and Hallstatt material cultures, and the relationship between ancient and modern Celtic identity address arts of Western peoples in antiquity and the Middle Ages.

The 12th-century Renaissance's Gothic architectural revolution, troubadour tradition, and contact with Islamic and Byzantine civilizations directly address arts of the Middle Ages in the Western tradition.
The Almoravid Empire across northwest Africa and Islamic Spain, its religious reformist ideology and cultural production, addresses arts of the Islamic peoples and North Africa.
Angkor Wat's extraordinary bas-reliefs depicting Hindu epics and its architectural significance in Southeast Asian art history directly address arts of Southeast Asian peoples.

Sappho as one of the greatest lyric poets of antiquity, her innovative use of the Sapphic stanza, and her reception from Roman poets through the Renaissance directly addresses arts of ancient Greece in the Western tradition.

Ovid's Metamorphoses and its vast influence on Western art from the Middle Ages through Shakespeare, Bernini, and Titian directly addresses arts in the Western tradition from antiquity through the Renaissance.
The Abbasid Caliphate's cultural golden age with achievements in arts and learning addresses arts of the Islamic peoples.
Indian mathematics spanning 5,000 years including the relationship between mathematics and Hindu-Buddhist cosmological thinking addresses the intellectual arts of South Asian peoples.
